entry.title: Static Rounding Profile
entry.content:
While managing customer orders in the system, in some process-based scenarios, it may be necessary to round the order quantity received from the customer due to requirements such as ensuring compatibility with package quantity arrangements. By assigning a specific coefficient, the entered order quantity can be rounded according to the relevant coefficient ratio. This rounding process can be carried out within the standard SAP structure by following the steps below.

Definition of Rounding Profile

Transaction code: OWD1

Configuration step: SPRO -> Material Management -> Purchasing -> Purchase Order Optimization -> Quantity Optimization and Available Logistic Units of Measure -> Maintenance of Rounding Profile


In the system, two different rounding profile types can be defined as shown in the image.

Static Rounding Profile

In Static Rounding Profile definition, a threshold value is set to perform basic rounding. The threshold value is defined at the plant level. No specific unit of measure is considered while defining the threshold value. When the entered order quantity exceeds the specified threshold, the system rounds it up to the next value. Once a rounding profile is defined for a plant, it can be used for different plants as well.

Dynamic Rounding Profile

In defining a dynamic rounding profile, besides considering the unit of measurement, it also offers different features. In defining dynamic profile, rounding method is selected and rounding rule is defined. In the rounding rule, percentage calculation is made on the basis of unit of measure. Similarly, the dynamic rounding profile is defined at the production site level and can be used at more than one production site.

"Static rounding profile" definition steps below:

"Threshold value" and "Rounding value" is selected.

Threshold value -> Order quantity that can be entered without rounding

Rounding value -> It is the value that should be rounded after the threshold value is passed. If the amount is entered over the rounding value, the system will perform the update process as multiples of the rounding value.

In the related example above, all orders entered over 1 quantity are rounded to 100.

Note: As mentioned above, the system does not take into account any unit of measure in the static rounding profile.

Item type rounding value configuration

Transaction code: VOV7

1) In order for rounding to be active on item type basis, the following item type maintenance is performed.

2) The rounding profile created in the system must be assigned in the material master.

In the above process, the rounding profile defined for the material.

3) As soon as the sales order is entered, the system revises the quantity by considering the rounding profile.

Simulation Process

There is also a "Simulation" button on the profile definition screen. The system simulates the quantity based on the rounding profile maintenance determined without even opening an order.

The system simulates that the value to be rounded for the entered amount is 100.

When the test is performed with a value above 100, the result is as follows;

The system simulates that the value to be rounded for the entered amount is 200.

entry.title: SAP Brownfield Migration: Successful transition to SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The transition to SAP S/4HANA is a crucial step in the digital transformation of companies. One possible migration strategy is brownfield migration , which involves converting the existing SAP ERP system to the new platform without redesigning processes and data from scratch.

But what challenges does a brownfield migration entail? A concrete example is the change in the length of material numbers , which requires companies to make technical and organizational adjustments.

What is an SAP Brownfield Migration?

SAP Brownfield Migration is a technical conversion process in which an existing SAP ECC system is migrated to SAP S/4HANA. Unlike Greenfield Migration , which involves building a new system, Brownfield Migration preserves existing processes, data, and custom code .

This strategy is suitable for companies that want to preserve their existing investments in the ERP system while benefiting from the advantages of SAP S/4HANA.

Challenges of Brownfield Migration: Example Material Number Length

One of the technical changes in SAP S/4HANA concerns the length of material numbers . In SAP ECC, material numbers were limited to 18 characters . With S/4HANA, this can be extended to 40 characters .

This change seems simple at first glance, but can have far-reaching consequences:

  1. Impact on custom code and in-house developments

Many companies have customized programs and reports that work with the existing material number length. Adjustments are necessary if:

  • In-house developments that have stored the material number as a fixed character length
  • ABAP programs work with manual character length restrictions
  • Reports and ALV lists are aligned to the old material number structure
  1. Adjustments in databases and interfaces

The extension of the material number can affect external systems, including:

  • Interfaces to suppliers or partners (EDI, B2B portals)
  • Merchandise management and warehouse management systems linked to SAP
  • Databases in which the material number is stored as a key
  1. Impact on printed forms and processes

Material numbers appear in many business processes, e.g.:

  • Invoices, orders and delivery notes
  • Product labels and packaging information
  • Workflows in which material numbers are used as reference values

Best practices for a successful brownfield migration

To ensure a smooth SAP Brownfield migration, companies should follow a structured approach :

  1. Analysis of the existing system landscape

Before migration, it's important to check which adjustments to custom code, data, and processes are necessary. SAP offers the Readiness Check , which identifies potential problems early on.

  1. Custom Code Adaptation

Existing ABAP code must be analyzed and adapted to be compatible with the new requirements of S/4HANA. This particularly applies to:

  • Database accesses with fixed field lengths
  • Selection masks and reports with material numbers
  • Programs that use material numbers for logic or comparison operations
  1. Data migration and cleansing
  • Checking whether material numbers in historical data are consistent
  • Cleanup and adaptation of old data structures
  • Ensure that external systems support the new material number length
  1. Testing and quality assurance
  • Conducting end-to-end tests to detect errors early
  • Training for users to ensure that the new processes are understood
  • Post-migration monitoring to quickly resolve performance and compatibility issues

Conclusion: Brownfield migration as a strategic decision

SAP Brownfield Migration is an effective way to transition to SAP S/4HANA with lower risk and lower cost than a Greenfield implementation. However, technical challenges such as material number length require detailed planning and careful adaptation of custom code and interfaces .

Companies should consider the impact of the migration early on and develop a structured migration and testing plan to ensure a smooth transformation.

Further information

Would you like to learn more about brownfield migration or receive customized consulting for your company? Contact us for a comprehensive analysis of your SAP system landscape.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM): A Basic Guide
entry.content:
Efficient returns management is a critical aspect of modern business operations, directly impacting customer satisfaction, operational efficiency, and financial performance. Traditional SAP return processes required separate workflows for customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns, often leading to manual interventions, inefficiencies, and high processing costs.

With SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM), SAP has unified these workflows into an automated, structured, and integrated framework. This blog provides a detailed walkthrough of ARM, covering core concepts, process flows, key advantages, and step-by-step configuration.

1. What is SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM)?

SAP Advanced Returns Management (ARM) is a return management solution embedded within SAP S/4HANA, designed to streamline end-to-end return processes across various business scenarios.

1.1 Key Benefits of ARM

  • Unified return processing for customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.
  • Automated document generation, reducing manual effort and errors.
  • Integration with SAP Quality Management (QM), Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), and Finance (FI/CO).
  • Dynamic follow-up actions based on return reasons and inspection results.
  • Enhanced visibility and tracking through SAP Fiori applications such as Manage Customer Returns.

 

2. Traditional SAP Returns Management vs. ARM

Key Differences Between Traditional Returns and ARM

Feature

Traditional SAP Returns

SAP S/4HANA ARM

Process Structure

Separate return processes for customer, supplier, and stock transfers

Unified, integrated return process

Document Flow

Requires manual handling

Automated follow-up document generation

Quality Management

Manual quality inspections

Automated QM integration with inspection lots

Tracking & Monitoring

Complex, manual tracking

Centralized tracking via Manage Customer Returns App

Automation

Manual document handling

Automated refund approvals and logistics execution

ARM reduces process complexity, eliminates redundancies, and improves end-to-end return cycle efficiency.

 

3. SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM) Processes

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management operates through three primary return processes: customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.

3.1 Customer Returns

Customer returns refer to the process initiated when a customer sends back a product for specific reasons. These returns can be classified as either planned or unplanned:

  • Planned Returns: Returns anticipated due to order errors, shipment issues, or product recalls.
  • Unplanned Returns: Returns initiated by the customer without prior notification or justification.

Customer Return Process Steps

  1. Creation of a Return Order (Return Sales Order)
  2. Generation of a Return Delivery Document (Return Delivery)
  3. Goods Receipt Processing (Goods Receipt)
  4. Quality Inspection and Follow-Up Actions (Quality Inspection & Follow-Up)
  5. Determination of Refund Amount and Execution of Customer Credit or Replacement (Refund or Replacement)

SAP Fiori's Manage Customer Returns application enables businesses to efficiently track and manage customer return orders.

 

ARM Configuration for Customer Returns

With the standard configuration complete, you’re ready to configure ARM for customer returns.

Here’s an overview of the most important steps:

➡️Activate ARM for return order types

You need to activate the sales document type to be used in ARM and assign subsequent document types for:

✔️Credit memo request

✔️Replacement order

✔️Delivery document

 

Step 1: Activate ARM for Return Order Types

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for Returns Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The CBAR sales document type is activated.

✔️Assigned subsequent document types.

 

Step 2: Activate ARM for CMR Order Types

📌Objective:

You need to activate the credit memo request sales document type to create a credit memo request during the refund process.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for CMR Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The GA2 sales document type is activated.

 

Step 3: Activate ARM for SDF Order Types

📌Objective:

The sales document type for replacement orders is used to create free-of-cost replacement orders in ARM.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for SDF Order Types

✅Action:

✔️The SD2 sales document type is activated.

 

Step 4: Configure, Activate, and Rename Follow-Up Activities

📌Objective:

You need to define logistics follow-up activities for processing return deliveries, which will drive the logistics flow of the return material.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

✅Action:

✔️Activate and rename follow-up activities used in ARM.

 

Step 5: Activate and Rename the Refund Control

📌Objective:

You need to activate refund control configuration, which will help in defining the controls on refund processing, such as:

✔️Process credit immediately

✔️No refund

✔️Refund through credit memo request processing

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Activate and Rename Follow-Up Activities

✅Action:

✔️Activate and rename refund control for an ARM return order.

 

Step 6: Define Return Reasons for Customer Returns

📌Objective:

You need to define return reasons for customer returns configuration, which will help in controlling the credit process (whether to give a refund or not) and other pricing elements.

📌Example:

✔️A restocking fee can be controlled based on the return reason.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Define Return Reasons for Customer Returns

✅Action:

✔️Several return reasons can be defined.

 

Step 7: Configure Default Values for Returns Refund Codes

📌Objective:

You need to configure default values for returns refund codes to control the automatic credit memo creation process for:

✔️Granting credit

✔️Rejecting credit based on inspection results provided by the quality team

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Configure Default Values for Returns Refund Codes

✅Action:

✔️Set up default refund codes to align with inspection results.

 

Step 8: Specify Follow-Up Document Types for Customer Returns

📌Objective:

You need to specify follow-up document types for customer returns to maintain:

✔️Automatic credit memo request processing

✔️Replacement orders

✔️Return stock/transfer orders

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Storage Locations for Follow-Up Activities

✅Assigned Document Types:

✔️NB2 → Supplier returns

✔️UB2 → Intracompany returns to another plant

✔️NB2C → Intercompany returns to another plant

✔️SD2 → SDF replacements

✔️GA2 → Credit memo requests

 

Step 9: Configure Movement Type for Goods Receipt Posting

📌Objective:

You need to specify the movement types for immediate goods receipt posting into various stock types, such as:

✔️Free stock

✔️Blocked stock

✔️Quality inspection stock

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Movement Types for Immediate Goods Receipts

✅Action:

✔️Maintain movement types for schedule line category AR.

 

Step 10: Specify Stock Type for Stock Transfers

📌Objective:

You need to specify the stock types, such as:

✔️Unrestricted stock

✔️Blocked stock

These stock types will be derived from the quality inspection result follow-up activity.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Sales and Distribution → Sales → Advanced Returns Management → Follow-Up Documents → Specify Stock Types for Goods Receipts

✅Action:

✔️Maintain plant-specific stock types.

 

Step 11: Map ERP Delivery Type to EWM Delivery

📌Objective:

To replicate outbound and inbound deliveries in the EWM system, you’ll need to map ERP delivery types to EWM delivery types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Ensure proper mapping of delivery document types for ARM returns.

 

Step 12: Map ERP Item Categories to EWM Item Types

📌Objective:

You also need to map ERP item categories to EWM delivery item types to ensure correct logistics execution.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Configure item category mappings between ERP and EWM for ARM return processes.

 

3.2 Supplier Returns

The process of returning products to the supplier due to quality issues or other reasons.

Supplier Return Process Steps:

  1. Create a Return Purchase Order (Return Purchase Order)
  2. Execute Return Shipment (Return Outbound Delivery & Goods Issue)
  3. Conduct Quality Inspection at the Supplier's End (Supplier Inspection)
  4. Process Supplier Credit or Replacement (Credit/Replacements from Supplier)

 

ARM Configuration for Supplier Returns

This section describes the configuration settings required for the ARM return purchase order process for a plant.

We assume that other Customizing settings (such as organizational structure, pricing, and output control) are already in place. You also need to maintain the configuration of document types across purchasing, shipping, inventory management, and invoice verification.

In addition to these configurations, you also need to perform the following mappings between embedded EWM and SAP S/4HANA (the ERP system).

 

Step: Set Up EWM for Supplier Returns

📌Objective:

To map the ERP delivery type and item category with the EWM document type and item type.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types

✅Action:

✔️Map ERP item categories with EWM item types via:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

 

3.3 Stock Transfer Returns

Stock transfer returns refer to return processes between different plants within the company.

Stock Transfer Return Process Steps:

  1. Create a Return Stock Transfer Order (Return Stock Transfer Order)
  2. Execute Shipment and Goods Issue (Outbound Delivery & Goods Issue)
  3. Goods Receipt and Quality Inspection at the Receiving Plant (Goods Receipt & Quality Inspection)
  4. Perform Relevant Follow-Up Actions (Follow-Up Actions)

 

ARM Configuration for Stock Transfer Returns

With the standard configuration complete, you’re ready to configure ARM for return stock transfer orders. Below is an overview of the most important steps:

 

Step 1: Activate the ARM Return Stock Transfer Order Document Type

📌Objective:

You can activate ARM for return stock transfer order document types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Advances Returns Management → Activate Advanced Returns Management for Purchase Order Types

✅Action:

✔️UB2 and NB2C purchase order types are activated for ARM.

 

Step 2: Define the Number Range for Advanced Returns Process IDs

📌Objective:

Define a number range for process IDs to enable process monitoring.

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Advances Returns Management → Define Number Range for Advanced Returns Process IDs

✅Action:

✔️The number range interval must be 01.

 

Step 3: Configure the Usage of Delivery Types for Store Returns

📌Objective:

Assign purchasing document category type F (purchase order) for document types UB2 and NB2C, as well as the plant (SPlt).

📌IMG Path:

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Returns Order → Store Return / Return Plant to Plant

✅Action:

✔️Configure delivery document types for store returns.

 

Step 4: Configure the Automatic Creation of Outbound Deliveries

📌Objective:

Set up automatic creation of outbound deliveries for return stock transfer orders.

📌IMG Path & Actions:

✔️Activate automatic replenishment deliveries & CRM billing

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Setup Stock Transport Order → Activate Automatic Delivery Creation and CRM Billing

✔️Activate or deactivate automatic outbound deliveries for stock transfer orders (per document type and shipping point)

➡️Materials Management → Purchasing → Purchase Order → Setup Stock Transport Order → Activate Automatic Delivery Creation for PO Type and Shipping Point

✔️Define profiles for creating and processing deliveries

➡️Logistics Execution → Shipping → Worklists → Maintain Parameters for Creating Deliveries

(SAP provides profile STOA, but custom profiles can be created.)

 

Step 5: Map ERP Delivery Type to EWM Delivery

📌Objective:

To replicate outbound and inbound deliveries in the EWM system, you need to map ERP delivery types to EWM delivery types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Ensure proper mapping of delivery document types for ARM return processes.

 

Step 6: Map ERP Item Categories to EWM Item Types

📌Objective:

To ensure correct logistics execution, you need to map ERP item categories to EWM delivery item types.

📌IMG Path:

➡️SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP to EWM

✅Action:

✔️Configure item category mappings between ERP and EWM for ARM stock transfer returns.

 

4. Integration with Quality Management

SAP S/4HANA’s Advanced Returns Management (ARM) solution is seamlessly integrated into customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns.

SAP has developed a straightforward approach to document material inspection in the ARM process, allowing users to enter inspection codes and comments.

Furthermore, ARM can be integrated with the Quality Management (QM) module in SAP S/4HANA for companies requiring detailed quality inspections to support quality assurance and process improvements in manufacturing industries.

How Quality Management is Integrated in ARM:

✔️Use an inspection lot to enter data for material inspection in ARM.

✔️Use a quality notification to:

  • Create a return order
  • Display a return order
  • Display a return order overview

This section will explore how quality management is integrated into returns processing, including its role in inspections and key configuration activities.

 

4.1 Quality Management in Returns Processing

In ARM, material inspection can be performed to accept or reject returned products.

  • Companies can choose to integrate ARM with Quality Management (QM) for automatic inspection lot creation.
  • The quality team inspects the product to determine the next steps, initiating a quality notification process for further analysis.
  • This helps companies diagnose product defects and make precise quality improvements.

 

Material Inspection Process in ARM

✔️A material inspection code can be entered at the time of return order creation.

✔️The inspection process is fast and optimized for retail and wholesale environments.

✔️A material inspection code can also be entered in the warehouse upon goods receipt for a return delivery.

Required Inspection Data in ARM:

To complete a material inspection, the following information must be recorded:

✔️Inspection code

✔️Inspection comments (long text)

✔️Material inspector details

✔️External document attachment

✔️Inspection date

When an inspection occurs at a customer location, the inspection results are typically entered at the time of return order creation.

📌To enter inspection results in the warehouse:

➡️SAP Easy Access → Logistics → Central Functions → Advanced Returns Management → Material Inspection → Enter Material Inspection in Warehouse

➡️Transaction Code: MSR_INSPWH

 

Key Fields in the Inspection Process

✔️Inspection Lot

  • The system updates the inspection lot number when activated.
  • Ad hoc inspections can be created by clicking the Create icon.

✔️Partial Lot

  • A unique number within the corresponding inspection lot.
  • Useful for batch splitting on the delivery note.

✔️Inspection Code

  • Defines the quality of the returned material as determined during inspection.

✔️Comment

  • Allows users to enter comments related to the inspection process.

✔️Inspection Date

  • Specifies the date when inspection was performed or recorded.

✔️Inspector

  • Identifies the person responsible for the inspection.

✔️Inspected Quantity

  • Defines the quantity of the inspected item or split item.

 

Conclusion

SAP S/4HANA Advanced Returns Management (ARM) provides a powerful, automated, and fully integrated solution for managing customer returns, supplier returns, and stock transfer returns efficiently.

In this blog, we covered the key configuration steps and main processes involved in ARM. However, this content serves as a summary, and further details may be required based on specific business needs.

For more in-depth insights or customized solutions, feel free to reach out to us!

📌Your feedback and questions are always welcome!

Thank you for reading! 😊🚀

entry.title: SAP Make-to-Order Insights
entry.content:
Today we will learn about the Make to order process used in SAP, the blog will give you an understanding of the business process and the configuration steps.

Strategies which businesses follow to produce goods are as follows:

Make to Order (MTO): The product is not manufactured in advance, Product is produced once the order is received, like Cars, Trucks, Bus’s etc.

Make to Stock (MTS): The product is manufactured in advance, it is produced in mass and fast moving in nature, such as consumer products, pharmaceuticals, household items, etc. 

 

 

Among these two production strategies we will discuss the Make to Order (MTO) business process in this blog.

Make to Order (MTO) Production Strategy:

"Make to order" refers to a production strategy where a company only manufactures a product once a customer places an order, meaning they don't produce goods in advance and stockpile inventory; this approach prioritizes customization and flexibility, allowing for tailored products based on individual customer needs, while minimizing the risk of overproduction and associated inventory costs, but often results in longer lead times for customers as production begins after the order is received.

Example- Cars, Trucks, bus’s etc.

Benefits:

  1. Reduce Inventory cost as production starts after order, saving on storage space of product.
  2. Increase customer satisfaction as they get what they want, Leads to higher satisfaction and loyalty.
  3. Greater flexibility as companies can adapt to changing market demands and quickly introduce new product variations based on customer feedback.
  4. Less waste as no overproduction.

Challenges:

  1. Potential for higher costs as it will be produced for special customers.
  2. Efficient production planning to meet customer deadlines.
  3. Longer lead time as production starts after receiving order. 

When to use?

  1. High production customization, when customer requires unique features or options on their products.
  2. Fluctuating demand: If customer demand is unpredictable and varies significantly, make to order can prevent overstocking. 
  3. High-value products: For expensive items minimizing inventory is crucial. 

Process flow for Make to Order:

Standard Configuration guide to activate MTO:

  1. Item category group: 0001/0002
  2. Item Category: TAK
  3. Schedule line category: CP
  4. MRP Type: ND/PD
  5. Special stock indicator: E
  6. Item category determination: OR + 0001+ Blank + Blank =TAK
  7. Strategy group:020
  8. Requirement Class: 040
  9. Requirement type: KE

Pre-requisites:

  •       In Material master settings that need to be carried out are as follow:
  • In Sales: Sales org. 2 screen enter Item Category group= 0001 (MTO) / 0002 for Configurable material.
  • In MRP1 screen, MRP type= PD/ND (No planning) & MRP controller = 101
  • In MRP3 screen, Strategy Group= 20 (Make to order/No assembly order).
  • In MRP3 screen, Availability check= 01 (Daily requirement).
  • Depending on business requirement you can create or use standard Sales document type, Item category & Schedule line category.

    Process Steps:

    • Create sales order

    Note: Costing for MTO process depends on Sales order, enter EK02 condition manually.

              
    • After creating sales order, we need to check whether the requirement has transferred to MRP using MD04.
    • Requirements planning in (MRP) is a manufacturing process control that includes production planning, scheduling, and stock requirement, enter transaction code: MD02, enter material, plant, MRP control parameters & execute it.
             
    • Convert planned order to Production order using MD04, Choose -> Prod. Order (Convert planned order into production order).
             
    • Release the Production order by clicking on flag and save the Prod. Order.
    • Withdrawing the Material for the Production Order (System creates a reservation for the relevant material components when you make a production order) using MIGO.
    • Confirm the production order using CO11N.
    • Create goods receipt against sales order using MIGO, Enter Movement type as 101.
    • Check the stock overview using MMBE.
    • The schedule lines will be confirmed in sales order, therefore create delivery & post goods issue.
    • Once PGI is done create Billing document.

Expected errors and their prospect solutions are as follow:

  • Error: Sales order does not trigger production order

Prospect solution: Assign correct requirement type to the item category and Ensure requirement class is defined.

  • Error: MRP does not generate planned orders.

Prospect solution: Check strategy group in material master and assign the correct requirement type.

  • Error: Production order does not consume sales order stock.

Prospect solution: Set special stock indicator as” E” in MRP 4 view of material master and ensure MRP settings align with MTO requirements.

  • Error: Sales order confirmation ignores component availability (ATP Not Considering Component Lead Times).

Prospect solution: Check ATP scope in OVZ9 and maintain proper AATP settings and ensure component lead times in material master.

Conclusion: Make-to-Order (MTO) process, stock can be customized, manufacturing can only begin only after receiving a confirmed customer order. Its primary advantages are lower inventory costs, greater customer satisfaction, enhanced production planning, and resource allocation efficiency.

entry.title: Why IDoc is Triggered Even After Setting Filters in BD64?
entry.content:
Introduction

Filtering IDocs in BD64 (Distribution Model) helps ensure only relevant data is sent to the target system. However, IDocs may still be generated despite applied filters, leading to confusion. This behavior aligns with standard SAP functionality.

Understanding the Issue

Even after setting filters in BD64, SAP may still trigger IDocs when filter conditions don’t match. This occurs due to:

  • Certain message types bypassing filters.
  • Configuration inconsistencies.
  • Standard SAP logic that processes IDocs before applying filters.

How to Prevent Unwanted IDocs

To avoid unnecessary IDoc generation:

  • Verify BD64 filter settings.
  • Check for SAP Notes addressing this behavior.

Where to check the segments is mandatory.

  • Go to Transaction code WE30.

  • Select objects name.

  •   After going inside the message type you can see this

 

  • Expand the Segments.

  • Double clicks the segments which you want to keep it as filter.

                 

 Key Consideration: Mandatory Checkbox in BD64

The Mandatory checkbox in BD64 plays a crucial role in filtering IDocs. SAP’s standard behavior removes non-mandatory segments but still allows IDoc transmission, which can lead to unexpected IDocs being generated.

Conclusion

If IDocs are created despite BD64 filters, it's due to this inherent SAP logic. To fully prevent unwanted IDoc generation, implement Badi IDOC_CREATION_CHECK as recommended in SAP Note 822929. This ensures only relevant IDocs are generated, optimizing data processing.

entry.title: Esri study shows HANA PCE in RISE supports 8x the target load in a benchmark
entry.content:
Many SAP customers are moving to RISE with SAP.  If you're reading this blog, you're probably familiar with my other blogs on SAP Community here.  If your moving your SAP ERP to RISE, your GIS system of record has to move to preserve performance and take advantage of simplified integration between S/4HANA and ArcGIS Enterprise.   Although Esri certified HANA as an enterprise geodatabase seven years ago, customers have been cautious - no utility wants to be the first one to put ArcGIS on the HANA platform.  With NiSource live in RISE with SAP with ArcGIS on HANA, now there is.

This past January, Esri ran a study that benchmarked the performance of a typical workflow mix (see the diagram below) against an ArcGIS Enterprise Utility Network geodatabase on an SAP HANA instance in the cloud.   The configuration, instance sizing, data and workflows are typical for an average mid-tier gas utility. Here are the characteristics of the dataset used in this study: a gas utility dataset with a geographic extent of roughly 55,000 square miles, and with an overall size of approximately 45 GB. The dataset contained roughly nine million features, 700 sub-networks, and 300,000 gas meters.

For some time, I worked with Esri to advocate running these studies and I'm very happy they have done them and made the results readily available here.  This blog unpacks the results of these studies and why it matters to any utility that uses ArcGIS Enterprise that wants to move to RISE. It also validates NiSource's decision to go live with ArcGIS on SAP HANA PCE last year.  For a thorough overview on this topic, read my blogs here on SAP Community on ArcGIS - SAP integration.  You can view them all here,

Here are the workflows used in all the HANA studies that Esri has run so far.  While the workflows below were running, workflows were run manually to completion while the system was under load.  This was done to observe any degradation of user experience.

With RISE with SAP, there's PCE (Private Cloud Environment) which means that the SAP landscape is run in its own tenant in one of the major hyperscalars.  Esri used AWS in this and previous studies.

The study ran two scenarios:

  1. An SAP HANA VM in the same VPC (Virtual Private Cloud) as the ArcGIS Enterprise related instances
  2. An SAP HANA VM running in the RISE PCE tenant which is a separate VPC from the ArcGIS Enterprise instances where the two VPCs are connected with AWS PrivateLink for this study.  It should be noted that @NiSource deployed in Azure and did not need to use an equivalent dedicated link in their production environment.

This study used VPCs that are within the same availability zone (AZ) and region.  That's the only way to ensure latency is kept to a minimum.

What were the results?

The test results show that as implemented, the systems had adequate physical resources to support usage from the design load through usage that was eight times the design load…. Both systems delivered similar performance, with no significant differences in workflow times or user experience.  There was also no meaningful change in hardware utilization between the two scenarios used in the test.  Workflow response times were acceptable and consistent all the way to eight times the design load.

These results show that utilities using SAP and ArcGIS can move their geodatabases to the cloud along with RISE with SAP and know that ArcGIS Enterprise with the geodatabase on the HANA platform will perform well - in fact up to 8 times the expected load

At eight times the design load for both scenarios:

  • Portal for ArcGIS and the hosting server CPU utilization generally stayed below 35% utilization
  • ArcGIS Server CPU utilization generally stayed below 60% utilization
  • SAP HANA CPU utilization (as reported) generally stayed below 45% utilization.  SAP provided these results obtained during the study to Esri for inclusion in this study.
  • Both scenarios provide a similar user experience with negligible differences in total conducted workflow time and step completion time.

Here are the graphs showing results for 8x the design load.

Aside from the performance and scalability afforded by the HANA platform, moving the geodatabase to the HANA platform means that access to your S/4HANA Plant Maintenance master and transactional data is on-demand; the data is always current.   

If you've going to RISE with SAP and want to leverage the capabilities of ArcGIS on HANA, this blog explains why your enterprise can confidently move SAP ERP to the cloud with RISE with SAP and move your ArcGIS Enterprise GIS system of record to the #HANA platform in the cloud.

Note: the screenshots are from the Esri study linked to in this blog.  All rights belong to their respective owners.

entry.title: Calculation View Features of 2025 QRC1
entry.content:
Within the time frame of 2025 Q1, several new calculation view features have been released in SAP Business Application Studio when connected to SAP HANA Cloud database QRC1. Some of these features are highlighted below. You can find examples that illustrate the individual features here. An overview of features of other releases can be found here.

Variables of Input Type Derived

With the new variable input type "Derived" filters can be constructed based on values that are returned in a tabular structure.

For example, a table function can serve as the source of all values that should be included in a filter. In the following screenshot a filter is constructed based on the returned values in column "filterValues" (2.) of tabular data source "filterTableNV" (1.). The filter is applied on column "city" (3.).

The resulting filter string could look like

 

"city" IN ( SELECT "filterValues" FROM "filterTableNV" )

 

 

The construction of the filter is the responsibility of the consuming analytics client and the user for executing the query requires privilege SELECT or EXECUTE for the tabular data source.
 
Use this option to flexibly construct filters at query execution time.
 

Copy Calculated/Restricted Columns and Input Parameters/Variables between Calculation Views

Calculated and restricted columns can be copied between different calculation views as long as the target node type matches the source node type.
Similarly, input parameters and variables can be copied.
 
If dependencies, such as used columns are missing in the target node a warning is shown and the dependency removed. If the dependency is mandatory the element has to be manually adapted.
 
More easily reuse your modelling developments across calculation views with this feature.
 

Clean Up Intermediate Data Preview Objects

Per default after using the intermediate data preview object option temporary objects for the intermediate data preview remain until the calculation view is redeployed. These objects can also be removed using the clean up option that is available during Data Preview With Other Database User:

Within this dialog the Clean Up option becomes available that allows to selectively remove remaining objects in the selected schema.

Remaining intermediate data preview objects prior to the database upgrade cannot be removed in this way and need a redeployment.

Remove remaining intermediate data preview objects after e.g., firefighting sessions

Choose Target Feature Version of a Project

Select which features should be available in the modeler for a given project. Only versions between the last frozen feature version and the current database release can be selected as target feature version. Selecting a feature version freezes it.

Use this option to keep available modeling features e.g., in development systems in sync with productive systems.

Support of Vector Engine Functions in Expressions

Vector engine functions such as VECTOR_EMBEDDING can now be used in expressions. VECTOR_EMBEDDING transforms data into a multidimensional vector based on a given model. Usage of the functionality requires the enablement of Natural Language Processing in the respective account.

Leverage the strength of HANA vector engine in expressions

Additional Execution Hints

With execution hint "conversion_result_type_downcast_after_aggregation" downcasting of currency converted measures to their target data type can be postponed until after the aggregation of the currency converted measure. This can be used to decrease rounding errors and to reduce resource consumption.

 

Execution hint "HIERARCHY_REMOVE_DUPLICATE_EDGES" controls whether duplicate edges that link a child node to the same parent node twice in a hierarchy are removed or not.

 
HIERARCHY_REMOVE_DUPLICATE_EDGES overwrites the default behavior. If set to TRUE also in case of shared hierarchies multiple edges are removed. If set to FALSE also in case of local hierarchies no removal of duplicate edges happens.
 
Increase control over calculation view execution by using hints if needed

entry.title: Mastering SPPD/SPAU/SPAU_ENH: Your Ultimate Guide to SAP Optimization
entry.content:
Why There is a Need for SPDD, SPAU, and SPAU_ENH

Introduction

SAP customers running on ECC 6.0 systems often streamline their business processes by customizing, modifying, or enhancing SAP standard system objects due to the unavailability of standard functionality. These changes need to be carried forward and adjusted during the transformation to new systems.

Types of Changes to SAP Standard Systems

  1. Customizing & Configuration: Setting system business parameters using special customizing transactions. Customizing is mandatory before using the SAP system in a live environment.
  2. Personalization & Adaptations: Tailoring the system to meet specific user needs without altering the underlying code.
  3. Enhancements Extensions & Integration: Customer changes to SAP repository objects without the need for modifications.
  4. Modification: Customer-specific changes to SAP repository objects. When SAP objects are changed, the customer version has to be modified to match the new version.

Understanding SPDD and SPAU

  • SPDD: Used to perform modification adjustments to certain ABAP dictionary objects such as domains, data elements, table structures, transparent tables, pooled tables, cluster tables, and table technical settings. Not performing modification adjustments for these objects could cause data loss.
  • SPAU: Used to perform modification adjustments for objects where not performing the adjustment would not directly lead to data loss. These objects include all other repository objects (such as module pools, ABAP programs, function modules, menus, and screens).
  • SPAU_ENH is a tool in SAP used during system upgrades to adjust enhancements made to the standard SAP system. When you upgrade your SAP system, some custom enhancements might not work correctly with the new version. SPAU_ENH helps you identify these enhancements and make the necessary adjustments so that everything continues to work smoothly. It's like a checklist that ensures all your custom tweaks are compatible with the upgraded system.

Adjusting Modified SAP Objects: SPDD, SPAU, and SPAU_ENH

When updating SAP systems, all modified standard SAP objects are displayed for adjustment in transactions SPDD, SPAU, or SPAU_ENH. Objects that SAP no longer delivers are deleted. If you want to keep these objects, you must accept the modifications in the respective transactions.

Important Considerations

  • Customer Namespace Package: Ensure there is at least one package in the customer namespace (Z*) before the update. This package is necessary if you need to create objects during the modification adjustment, such as an append structure for customer fields of an SAP table. Packages cannot be created during the upgrade.
  • Data Loss Prevention: If deleted ABAP Dictionary objects are not manually re-created during SPDD, data loss may occur. For example, this can happen if a structure is used as a table append.

Adopting Existing Modifications and Changes in SAP: A Step-by-Step Guide

Step-by-Step Guide

  1. Review Each TAB Object in SPDD/SPAU/SPAU-ENH:
    • Go through each object listed in the SPDD, SPAU, and SPAU_ENH transactions.
  2. Download Lists:
    • Download the complete SPDD, SPAU, and SPAU_ENH lists into an Excel file.
    • Prepare an action plan based on these lists.
  3. Analyze and Compare Versions:
    • Based on the project guidelines, adopt the changes.
    • Analyze the changes by comparing the versions and propose actions to be taken in the Excel file.
  4. Execute Actions:
    • Once the review is complete and new actions are confirmed, start executing the actions.
  5. Documentation:
    • Keep track of all activities performed using proper documentation.

Options to Adopt Standard Changes

Options

 

  1. Notes: Implementing SAP Notes to apply corrections and updates.
  2. With Assistant: Using SAP tools and assistants to guide you through the process.
  3. Without Assistance: Manually adopting changes without the help of automated tools.
  4. Deletions, Translations, etc.: Handling deletions and translations as part of the change adoption process.

Available Actions

 

 

 

  1. Reset - Latest Version

Purpose: Reverting to the latest version of the object provided by SAP. Use Case: When you want to discard all custom modifications and use the standard SAP version. 

Steps:

  • Select the object in SPAU.
  • Choose the "Reset to Original" option.
  • Confirm the reset action.
  1. Adjust - Mix of Latest and Old Changes

Purpose: Combining the latest changes from SAP with existing custom modifications. Use Case: When you want to retain some custom modifications while incorporating new SAP updates. 

Steps:

  • Review the differences between the current version and the new SAP version.
  • Manually merge the changes, ensuring both custom and new SAP changes are preserved.
  • Test the adjusted object to ensure functionality.
  1. Adjust with Proposal – Recommendation

Purpose: Adopting changes based on system recommendations. Use Case: When SAP provides a recommended way to adjust the object, often through automated tools. 

Steps:

  • Select the object in SPAU.
  • Choose the "Adjust with Proposal" option.
  • Review the proposed changes and confirm the adjustments.
  1. Adjust Manually – Manual

Purpose: Manually adjusting the changes as needed. Use Case: When automated proposals are not suitable, and manual intervention is required. 

Steps:

  • Compare the current version with the new SAP version.
  • Manually edit the object to incorporate necessary changes.
  • Test the object thoroughly to ensure it works correctly.
  1. Change Processing Status

Purpose: Manually changing the status of the object when required. Use Case: When you need to update the status of an object to reflect its current state (e.g., "post-processing required" or "For testing").

 Steps:

  • Select the object in SPAU.
  • Open the dialog to change the processing status.
  • Update the status and assign processors if needed.
  1. Mark for Testing

Purpose: Keeping track of objects that need to be tested. Use Case: When an object requires testing before it can be confirmed as finished. 

Steps:

  • Select the object in SPAU.
  • Assign tester roles to the processors.
  • Reset confirmation flags to ensure the object is marked for testing.
  1. Delete Modification Log

Purpose: Removing the log of modifications. Use Case: When a modified development object is deleted by the software delivery, and the modification log needs to be managed. 

Steps:

  • Select the object in SPAU.
  • Choose the option to delete the modification log.
  • Confirm the deletion, and the object will no longer be listed after a restart.
  1. Retrieve Last Version

Purpose: Restoring the last version of the object. Use Case: When you need to revert to the previous version of the object before the latest changes were applied. 

Steps:

  • Select the object in SPAU.
  • Choose the "Retrieve Last Version" option.
  • Confirm the action to restore the previous version.

 

Deletion Objects

Change Processing Status

When a development object has not yet been confirmed as finished by at least one assigned processor, it remains in the status "post-processing required" or "For testing".

  • Assigning Processors: If you are not already assigned, you will be added as a processor of the object. A dialog will open, allowing you to add or remove processors, change their roles, and update assessments.

Mark for Testing

If a development object has not been confirmed as finished by at least one assigned processor, it remains in the status "post-processing required".

  • Assigning Tester Roles: All assigned processors will be given the role of "Tester". The confirmation flags will be reset, ensuring that the object is marked for testing.

Delete Modification Log

When a modified development object is deleted by the software delivery, the modification log needs to be managed.

  • Deleting Logs: The modification log will be deleted, and SPAU will no longer list the object after a restart.

Managing SAP Translations

Adjusting a Translation

  • Issue: Translations overwritten or deleted by SAP transport.
  • Action: Adjust modifications first if original text isn't created by SAP. Save changes in SPAU or use SE63 if navigation fails.

Resetting Translation for a Single Language

  • Issue: Translations overwritten or deleted by SAP transport.
  • Action: Reset translations if no longer needed or new ones are adequate. Mark as no longer modified; existing translation remains.

Marking Translation for a Single Language as Adjusted

  • Issue: Translations overwritten or deleted by SAP transport.
  • Action: Adjust modifications first if original text isn't created by SAP. Add or change translations, then mark as adjusted.

entry.title: Dynamic select query using source syntax
entry.content:
Introduction:

This blog intends dynamic fields or table selection in select query based on the condition. My requirement is to show vendor or customer details based on T-code. A traditional approach is to use conditional statement (IF .. ELSE)  to determine fields, table and where condition. However, there is an alternative and dynamic approach by utilizing source syntax which eliminates reductant code

Step 1: Declare type as below image.

Step 2: Declare internal table as common for both vendor or customer details.
Note: Inline declaration in select query is not possible in source syntax

Step 3: Map the structure(ls_source_syntax) based on T-Code

  1. Column - Based on T-code map the required fields.
  2. Source - Based on T-Code map the required table.
  3. Where – based on T-Code map the selection option

Step 4: Dynamically fields, table, where get mapped based on T-code as shown in the image

Case 1: T-Code: ZLIFNR – Vendor details for particular vendor.

Step1: Pass the input and execute

Output:

 

Case 2: T-Code: ZKUNNR – Customer details

Step1: Pass the input and execute

Output:

Conclusion:

With the source syntax, we can construct select query dynamically based on our requirement to make more flexible instead of traditional approach.

entry.title: SAP Developer News March 27th, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

PODCAST: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/03/27/sap-developer-news-march-27th-2025/ 

New SAP Enterprise Architecture learning journey 

  • "Discovering SAP Enterprise Architecture" learning journey https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/discovering-sap-enterprise-architecture-portfolio  

Good To Know YouTube Shorts on ABAP Cloud 

  • Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAd1pKjxrPpkOwia5RFw0DE 

What’s new in SAP HANA Cloud Q1 2025  

  • Detailed blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-sap-hana-cloud-march-2025/ba-p/14042046  

Cloud Integration and connectivity 

  • EOIO in JMS adapter: https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configure-jms-sender-adapter and https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?Valid_as_Of=2025-03-16:2025-03-16&locale=en-US&Component=Cloud+Integration 
  • Generative AI-Based Script Optimization - Groovy scripts: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/generative-ai-based-script-optimization/ba-p/13970403  
  • Cloud Connector 2.18 is out: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-cloud-connector-2-18-is-out/ba-p/14049553  
  • Cloud Connector release notes: https://help.sap.com/whats-new/cf0cb2cb149647329b5d02aa96303f56?Component=Connectivity&locale=en-US&Valid_as_Of=2025-03-06:2025-03-06 

 

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 New SAP Enterprise Architecture learning journey 

0:59 GoodToKnow YouTube Shorts on ABAP Cloud 

1:41 What’s new in SAP HANA Cloud Q1 2025 

5:21 Cloud Integration and connectivity 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for March 27th, 2025.  

[DJ] I've had many roles in my working life in the SAP Technical Ecosphere. One of those roles was as an SAP Enterprise architect. So when I saw Stefan highlight a new learning journey all about SAP Enterprise architecture, my interest was piqued. As Stefan highlights, it's a core starts, beginners -friendly and covers everything you need to get started with SAP Enterprise Architecture, including what the role entails, fundamentals, key concepts, tools, frameworks and so on, plus a look at SAP's reference architecture. The course is free, so what are you wasting for? Happy learning.  

[Shilpa] Hey, ABAP developers, you might have come across good-to-know YouTube short shots, on ABAP Cloud, which we publish every Friday since the beginning of this year. So far, we have covered 12 videos and there are many more to come. We are covering in two segments. Firstly, about ABAP development tools. If you're new to ADT, these tips and tricks will definitely help you into smooth transitioning into ADT. Secondly, some good facts and points about ABAP cleaner. You can configure ABAP cleaner, which is a tool intended to automate as much as possible with respect to ABAP code style. The link to the playlist is in the description. Check it out and do let us know what do you think.  

[DJ] It is me again, DJ, this time though, standing in for Witalij, my good friend and colleague, because this item is all about SAP Hana Cloud. So there's always innovation in that space. And to mark the end of March, Thomas Hammer has published a blog post highlighting what's new in the SAP Hana Cloud Q1 2025 release. And my goodness me, it's massive. It's a bumper edition. So I thought I'd take some time to summarize the details of the Q1 release, so you know what you have to look forward to. You can get an overview of the main ones in a four-minute video that Thomas links from his blog post, but the detail breakdown you can get from the blog post itself. So Thomas divides up the new. features into different sections. The first one being administration and service management. Basically, there is improved instance management and generation. And there's also better issue management and diagnosis through the automatic summarization of issues. In the machine learning and predictive analytics area, there are facilities to improve machine learning, That's through better regression model building and unified classification. There's also support for text extraction from binary files such as PDF, for example. And there's also a new generative AI toolkit with lots of new features. In the multi-model data processing arena, there's now native support for the resource description framework, RDF, and therefore also sparkle as well as SQL. There's better maximum flow graph problem support. There's also an addition of a new alter vector index statement, which means you can keep your indices up to date, which is great for performance. And there's also support for vector data storage using the native storage extension. In the area of graphical view modeling, There are better facilities for cleaning up intermediate data previews. There's a new facility for using tables views or functions to derive multiple values from input data. There's a new execution hint in the area of currency conversion performance and a new vector embedding function. There's also, last but not least, the ability to target a specific database release when creating calculation views. Beyond all that, there's a ton of innovations also in SAP Hana Cloud Data Lake. There are expanded data availability, data center rather, availability for SAP Cloud and lots of improvements in the area generally of database application development. This was just the briefest of brief summaries. I hope I've done Thomas's blog post justice. I encourage you strongly to go and check out Thomas's blog post and see you next time. 

 [Antonio] Holla, SAP developers. I got some exciting needs to share with you. In Cloud Integration, you can now complete your JMS Queue to either exclusive or non-exclusive access types, meaning that we now have exactly once in order of processing for JMS. With the non-exclusive access type, messages are processed concurrently. With the exclusive access type, messages are processed sequentially by a single consumer and in the order in which they are received. I'm including a link to the documentation in the description. Also, there's a new generative AI feature available in cloud integration. It was announced at SAP Tech at last year. In essence, we're now able to optimize our groovy scripts. The optimized script   functionality will generate a report, which will give you a general assessment of the script. It will also recommend some changes, if applicable, and even provide you with an improved script. Remember that to use this new feature, you will need to have enabled the Gen AI-based features in your tenant. Now let's talk a bit about connectivity. There's a new version of Cloud Connector available. Version 2.18, apart from a couple of bug fixes, there are quite a few enhancements. For example, it now supports Windows Server 2025. There's also support for automatic renewal of the subaccount certificate. It introduces a new centralized management of email and LDAP server trust stores. There's a new troubleshooting section to help you diagnose problems within the Cloud Connector and much more. Make sure to check out the release notes link that's included in the description. Ciao.

entry.title: Automating SAP HANA Cross SID Restore with AWS Backint and Web UI
entry.content:
Background

AWS Backint for SAP HANA was introduced as a certified backup and restore solution for SAP HANA databases running on AWS. It has simplified the process of backing up SAP HANA databases directly to Amazon S3, leveraging AWS's robust and scalable storage infrastructure. AWS backint backup integrates seamlessly with HANA Cockpit for automated backup scheduling and lifecycle management, reducing the administrative overhead associated with manual backup processes. However, cross SID restore, as a critical part of any system refresh, e.g. from production to quality assurance, still involves several manual steps, making it time-consuming and prone to errors. In this blog, we will leverage AWS Backint and Web UI to significantly streamline and automate this process, enhancing efficiency and reliability.

Challenges in SAP HANA Backint Restore Cross SID

Though AWS backint backup and same database restore are integrated with HANA Cockpit and HANA Studio, cross SID backint restore has stayed as a manual process at the time of writing. For cross SID restore with backint, the following manual steps must be performed:

  • Find Backup ID from Source Database:

Identify the specific backup ID from the source SAP HANA database that you wish to restore. We will introduce autoscanning method to upload backup metadata to SQLite database to make it easy to select the right backup ID.

  • Modify YAML File :

Adjust the YAML file associated with backint on the S3 bucket and folder on the target database. e.g. For yaml file,  /hana/shared/aws-backint-agent/aws-backint-agent-config.yaml, the following parameters need adjustment as the source bucket and folder may be different from the target.
   S3BucketName: "xyz-backint-prd"
   S3BucketFolder: "backup"

Moreover, after the restore process is completed, ensure to revert the changes, 

  • Execute Complex HANA SQL Command:

Perform HANA SQL commands within hdbsql of the target database to initiate the restore process. The SQL for the restore is long and could be confusing with various SIDs and paths. 

As seen above, it could be daunting to carry out the restore process. It often requires the collaboration of both infrastructure and basis teams to ensure accuracy and success. The manual nature of these steps increases the risk of errors and can be time-consuming to troubleshooting.  It calls for a more automated and streamlined solution. 

Solution Overview

We propose a solution that leverages:

  • Streamlit: For developing a user-friendly Web UI.
  • AWS SSM: For managing SAP systems and automating tasks.
  • SQLite: For storing SAP system info and backup metadata

We aim to simplify the restore process. Only a few clickings will be all that is needed to complete the AWS backint restore cross SID.

Prerequisites

Before we dive into the implementation, ensure you have the following:

  • Access to AWScli which can operate on HANA servers via SSM.
  • Basic knowledge of Streamlit and SQLite.
  • Python installed on your system.
  • HANA DB passwords are stored into AWS secrets manager.
  • Target DB instance can read from the source S3 bucket. 

Some Conventions in HANA

To help us identify source and target. we will adopt the following concepts and conventions commonly used in HANA literature.

  • HANA System ID (SID): The HANA System ID (SID) is a unique identifier for an SAP HANA system. It is typically 3 characters in upper case, such as PRD, QAS and DEV.
  • HANA SYSTEMDB: The central database managing system-wide operations and configurations. It is automatically created during the installation of the SAP HANA system.
  • HANA Tenant DB: Independent databases within the SAP HANA system, each serving specific applications or clients while sharing system resources. There could be several tenant DBs, but each of them will be typically identified by 3 characters, e.g. PR1, PR2, PRD.

In order to specify HANA tenant database exactly, we will have to identify it in the context of HANA system. For Example, 

  • PR1@PRD meaning Tenant DB PR1 as part of PRD HANA system
  • PRD@PRD meaning Tenant DB PRD as part of PRD HANA system

We may identify SYSTEMDB in the context of HANA system too, e.g.  SYSTEMDB@PRD to mean the SYSTEMDB of PRD HANA System.

HANA Backint Restore Tool

We have developed HANA Backint Restore Tool for SAP system refresh.

Once we select the source database and target database, the tool will automatically fetch the backup ID list along with the time of backup.  The backup records have been prefetched in SQLite database table.

It is easy to choose the Backup ID based on the date / time of the backup.

Once source, target and backup ID are selected, the Restore Tool will figure out the full roadmap to proceed.  All we need is :    Click to Proceed to Restore !

What is inside the button "Click to proceed with restore" and other buttons

Each of the buttons is called by Streamlit to run AWScli SSM document, namely  "AWS-RunShellScript". 

To come up the syntax of the CLI, we can follow the following procedures.

Go to SSM Run Command, and choose the document "AWS-RunShellScript" 

 

Enter the bash commands to execute, e.g. in case of updating YAML,

cd /hana/shared/aws-backint-agent/;
sed -i.bak -e '/S3Bucket/s/backint-qas/backint-prd/' aws-backint-agent-config.yaml

Select the instance corresponding to the target database. To automate the process, we have already pre-scanned the instance id and HANA SID/DB information in our SQLite database.

The CLI collected here is what we use in our tool.

The same goes for calling hdbsql commands to stop tenant DB and cross SID restore. By the way, the systemdb password will be extracted from secrets manager and used to login to hdbsql.

Once restore is kicked off, we can click to check the restore status. If done, we can proceed to finish post steps within SAP.  Finally, come back to the tool to revert change on YAML. Then normal operations can resume.

Conclusion

Automating the Cross SID restore process using AWS Backint and Web UI transforms a traditionally complex and manual task into a streamlined, efficient, and reliable operation. By leveraging these tools, organizations can smooth out system refreshes, enhancing overall operational efficiency.

References

https://aws.amazon.com/backint-agent/

Streamlining SAP Operations and Monitoring with SSM-SAP and Streamlit

Appendix

We have many scripts in various parts of our restore tool. Here we illustrate some of them.

SQLite 

Assume we use sqlite3  --- current at the writing. The following will open or create database backup.db as a file.

  sqlite3 backup.db

Once inside, use the following command to create backup records table:

create table backup_records (backup_id string, sid string, tid string, backup_time string, primary key (backup_id) );

Also use this command to collect backup id. Will need to go to the directory with backup.log first.

egrep 'BACKUP[ ]*backup ID' backup.log |sed 's?usr/sap/\(.*\)/SYS/.*backint/\(.*\)/202? \1 \2 ?'| awk '{printf("insert into backup_records values (\047%s\047,\047%s\047,\047%s\047, \047%s\047);\n", substr($8,1,length($8)-1), $12, $13, $1)}'|uniq

Run the resulting SQL to insert to the table inside SQLite. 

Streamlit

We illustrate with a simple selection function for backup ID.  

 

import sqlite3
import streamlit as st
import pandas as pd
import os

def run_query():
   
    st.write("### Select Backup to be restored")

    conn = sqlite3.connect('backup.db')
    cursor = conn.cursor()

    query = "SELECT * FROM backup_records"
    cursor.execute(query)
    results = cursor.fetchall()
    column_names = [description[0] for description in cursor.description]

    df = pd.DataFrame(results, columns=column_names)

    selected_label = st.radio(
        "Choose the backup to restore:",
        df.index.map(lambda x: f"BACKUP_ID {df.loc[x, 'backup_id']}: {df.loc[x, 'backup_time']}"),
        format_func=lambda x: x
    )

    if selected_label:
        # Extract the index from the label
        selected_index = int(selected_label.split()[1][:-1])
        selected_row = df[df['backup_id'] == selected_index].iloc[0]
    else:
        st.info("No row selected.")
    conn.close()

    backup_id = selected_row.backup_id
    st.write("Backup ID selected: "+ str(backup_id))
 
tool_selection = {
    "SAP Refresh: HANA Backint Restore Tool": run_query
}

selected_page = st.sidebar.selectbox("Choose a SAP Toolkit for AWS", tool_selection.keys())
tool_selection[selected_page]()

It is still a plan to share the codes for monitoring and restore after code cleanup. Appreciate the patience.

entry.title: Exporting SAP Table Data as CSV and ZIP on Application Server using ABAP - A Comprehensive Guide
entry.content:
​In many SAP projects, there's a frequent need to extract master data or transaction data from the system for purposes such as reporting, data migration, or integration with external systems. A common approach involves exporting this data into a CSV file, compressing it into a ZIP archive, and saving it on the application server for efficient storage and transfer.

This blog post presents an ABAP report that automates the extraction of data from the SFLIGHT table, generates a CSV file, compresses it into a ZIP file, and stores both files on the application server. This solution is particularly useful for scenarios requiring data extraction and compression for streamlined processing.

Business Scenario

Assume you have a requirement to export a list of flight bookings to share with an external partner or to store for auditing purposes. However, due to data sensitivity or storage limitations, the file needs to be compressed before sharing. This ABAP report handles:

  • Reading data from SFLIGHT table
  • Creating a well-formatted CSV file
  • Zipping the file using CL_ABAP_ZIP
  • Writing both CSV and ZIP to the application server

Process Flowchart

Here's an overview of the key steps involved:

  1. Start - Export Process

  2. User Input: File Path & Name

  3. Select Data from SFLIGHT Table

  4. Create CSV String

  5. Save CSV File to Application Server

  6. Convert to XSTRING

  7. Create ZIP using CL_ABAP_ZIP

  8. Save ZIP File to Application Server

  9. Show Success/Error Messages

  10. End - Export Process

 

ABAP Code Sample:  

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report YYCSVZIP
*& Author: Shravankumar Tiwari *
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT yycsvzip.

*** Data Declaratio **
DATA:
  gv_filename     TYPE string,
  gv_filename_zip TYPE string,
  gv_filepath     TYPE string,
  gv_csv_string   TYPE string,
  gv_zip_path     TYPE string,
  gv_xstring      TYPE xstring,
  gv_o_zip        TYPE REF TO cl_abap_zip. "Zip Utility

*** Constant declaration
CONSTANTS gc_zip TYPE char04 VALUE '.zip'.

* Selection screen
PARAMETERS:
  p_path  TYPE string OBLIGATORY DEFAULT '/tmp/' LOWER CASE,
  p_fname TYPE string OBLIGATORY DEFAULT 'A_sflight_export' LOWER CASE ##NO_TEXT.

* Exception handling
DATA:
  gs_x_bcs TYPE REF TO cx_bcs.

START-OF-SELECTION.
* Select data from SFLIGHT
  SELECT carrid,connid,fldate,price,currency
    FROM sflight
    INTO TABLE
    (gt_sflight) UP TO 50 ROWS
    ORDER BY PRIMARY KEY.                               "#EC CI_NOWHERE

* Prepare CSV header
  gv_csv_string = 'CARRID,CONNID,FLDATE,PRICE,CURRENCY'.

* Add data rows
  LOOP AT gt_sflight ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<gs_sflight>).
    gv_csv_string = gv_csv_string &&
      cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline &&
      |{ <gs_sflight>-carrid }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-connid }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-fldate }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-price }| &&
      |,{ <gs_sflight>-currency }|.
  ENDLOOP.

* Prepare filename and path
  gv_filename = p_fname && sy-datum && sbiw_c_underscore && rsbo_c_suffix-csv.
  gv_filepath = p_path  &&  gv_filename.

* Write CSV file
  OPEN DATASET gv_filepath FOR OUTPUT IN TEXT MODE ENCODING DEFAULT.
  IF sy-subrc = 0.
    TRANSFER gv_csv_string TO gv_filepath.
    CLOSE DATASET gv_filepath.
  ELSE.
    MESSAGE 'File could not be created' TYPE 'E' ##NO_TEXT.
  ENDIF.

* Create ZIP file
  CREATE OBJECT gv_o_zip.
  gv_filename_zip = p_fname && sy-datum && sbiw_c_underscore.
  gv_zip_path = p_path && gv_filename_zip && gc_zip.

  TRY.
* Convert string to xstring with explicit exception handling
      gv_xstring = cl_bcs_convert=>string_to_xstring(
        iv_string   = gv_csv_string
        iv_codepage = '4103'     " UTF-8 codepage
        iv_add_bom  = 'X'        " Add Byte Order Mark
      ).
*ADD Adds a File to a Zip Folder
      gv_o_zip->add( name    = gv_filename_zip
                     content = gv_xstring ).

*SAVE Creates a Zip File
      DATA(gv_zip_path_data) = gv_o_zip->save( ).

* Write ZIP file on apllication server
      OPEN DATASET gv_zip_path FOR OUTPUT IN BINARY MODE.
      IF sy-subrc = 0.
        TRANSFER gv_zip_path_data TO gv_zip_path .
        CLOSE DATASET gv_zip_path.
      ELSE.
        MESSAGE 'Zip File could not be created' TYPE 'E' ##NO_TEXT.
      ENDIF.

** Success messages Output
      DATA(gv_file_message) = | { gv_filepath } { cl_abap_char_utilities=>newline } { gv_zip_path }|.
      cl_demo_output=>display( gv_file_message ).


    CATCH cx_bcs INTO gs_x_bcs.
      " More detailed logging approach
      MESSAGE ID 'ZZZ' TYPE 'E' NUMBER 000
        WITH 'BCS Conversion Error' ##NO_TEXT
        RAISING error ##MG_MISSING.
  ENDTRY.

Example Output

Assuming default parameters and today’s date is 20250331, the output files would be:

  • CSV: /tmp/A_sflight_export20250331_.csv

  • ZIP: /tmp/A_sflight_export20250331_.zip

 

Conclusion

This ABAP code demonstrates how standard ABAP can be used efficiently for file handling, CSV generation, and ZIP compression — all without any third-party tools. It’s ideal for creating reports, system integrations, or any scenario requiring lightweight file exports.

entry.title: Where can I find learning materials on how to apply the machine learning workflow?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Developing Classification Models with the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA learning journey.

Overview
In this learning journey, you will explore how to apply the machine learning workflow. The steps from feature extraction to model evaluation are illustrated to ensure a comprehensive understanding of the process. The scenario presented in this learning journey focuses on preventing employee churn, a key business problem addressed using SAP HANA Cloud Machine Learning.

Learning objectives
By the end of this learning journey, you will be able to:

  • Configure the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA (hana-ml) and connect it to SAP HANA Cloud.
  • Access and visually explore SAP HANA data using HANA DataFrames in Python.
  • Train a classification model for predicting employee churn rate using hana-ml Predictive Analysis Library (PAL) functions.
  • Perform model evaluation to assess the accuracy and effectiveness of the trained model.

Prerequisites

If you need some foundational information, you can also refer to
Developing AI Models with the Python Machine Learning Client for SAP HANA 

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: SAP Developer News April 3rd, 2025
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION 

Podcast: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/04/03/sap-developer-news-april-3rd-2025/ 

Developer Challenge 

  • Blog: TK 
  • SAP Community Escape House: https://sap-community-escape-house.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/house 

 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation  

  • Blog : https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-brings-generative-ai-to-process-automation-to-enable-your/ba-p/14049869  
  • SAP Help Portal : https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation/sap-build-process-automation/generate-process?locale=en-US  

 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/create-sap-build-apps-applications-in-minutes-with-ai/ba-p/14054862 
  • SAP Help portal https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/generate-pages-using-ai 

 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

  • Registration form: https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=bGf3QlX0PEKC9twtmXka9zoyeoeObGdNh1zA5HmpzP5UMEVIN0xWUlBRWUlCWUlQUVJKV1pZMDFBWS4u  

  

CHAPTER TITLES  

0:00 Intro 

0:10 Developer Challenge 

1:12 Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation 

2:08 Generative AI in SAP Build Apps 

3:08 SAP BTP Usability Event – HANA Cloud 

 

TRANSCRIPTION

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for April 3rd, 2025.

[Daniel] You might have heard the sound of heavy machinery working on an addition to the SAP community escape house. Well, I'm happy to say that the SAP build garage is now ready. April's Developer Challenge will deal with a specific and key feature of SAP build. Well, I can tell you that. You have to discover that for yourself. It will include spy gear, exercise equipment, something from last month's AI challenge, and a few things that you won't see when you first enter the room. It will be a real challenge, no doubt about it. But if you're able to get out of the garage alive, please add a comment to my blog post and tell me how it went. Good luck.

[Rekha DR] Hello everybody! Are you a developer using SAP Build tools to build applications and automations? Then here is a good news for you to amplify productivity and efficiency in your day-to-day work. You can now use Generative AI in SAP Build Process Automation. You can enable Generative AI for your BTP tenant within the control tower of SAP. and then you can use Generative AI to generate a business process, a data type or a form from the overview screen of the project. Within the process, you can create an automation task, explain the business process in natural language. Generate new code in the script task, explain a decision or generate business rules. For more details, check this blog post by product expert, Yogananda Muthaiah provided in the description box. Keep learning, keep exploring. Thank you.  

[Shrinivasan] SAP applications are continuing to evolve with generative AI capabilities, and these capabilities are now available in SAP Build Apps. There is a new AI powered feature available in our no-code development environment now. SAP Build Apps now includes a generative AI feature that makes it easier to create pages for create, read, update operations on you. your existing OData backup. Simply enable an identity from the Integrations tab, and you will see the new Generate Pages option. Keep in mind, you will need to enable the Generative AI feature from the Control Tower first to use this option. Once generated, these pages can be customized and extended further to suit your specific needs. Note that the generated Pages feature comes with a usage limit of 100 requests per month for each user. Explore this new functionality to to jumpstart your application development. The links to the blog post and the help portal on this new feature is available in the description.

[Antonio] Holla, SAP Database Administrators and Database Developers. Have you ever wanted to provide feedback on the SAP HANA Cloud database service? Well, now you can. There will be an SAP BTP usability event on the 13th and 14th of May in Walldorf Germany. This is an in-person event where you can gain insights on the SAP's product vision. Also, you can share ideas directly with the development teams and provide your input on new features directly to the engineering teams. If you're interested in participating, make sure to register on the link that I'm going to share in the description of this video. Thank you very much for your time.Ciao

entry.title: Shaping the Future with Data and AI: SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine and Generative AI Toolkit
entry.content:
We’re pleased to announce the general availability of two groundbreaking technologies: the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine and the Generative AI Toolkit. These solutions are designed to elevate your business intelligence and AI capabilities, offering enhanced data connectivity, smarter insights, and faster decision-making.

This blog post highlights the features of these new offerings and showcases how they align with SAP’s long-standing commitment to deliver innovations that drive your business forward. More importantly, it’s our way of saying: We’ve kept our promise.
